question: How does the power density of the GB200 NVL72 compare to a DGX H100 system?
findings: The GB200 NVL72 rack incorporates 36 NVIDIA Grace CPUs, 72 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, up to 17 TB of LPDDR5X memory, up to 13.5 TB of HBM3E, and a 130 TB/s NVLink domain.
The GB200 NVL72 rack includes 3× 1U 33 kW power shelves on each side (6 total), uses a fully liquid-cooled design, and compute trays feature 2× Superchip cold plate loops and 8× fans per tray.
Each GB200 NVL72 compute tray contains 4 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs (192 GB HBM3E each) and 4× 400 Gb/s OSFP ConnectX-7 NICs.
The HGX H100 (DGX H100 base platform) uses 8× NVIDIA H200 SXM GPUs with 1.1 TB total memory, 900 GB/s per-GPU NVLink bandwidth, and 7.2 TB/s total NVLink bandwidth via fourth-generation NVLink.
The GB200 NVL72 delivers 30× higher LLM inference performance and 4× higher LLM training performance compared to NVIDIA HGX H100.

research_gaps.description: No power delivery or PDU infrastructure guidance found.
No UPS or battery backup requirements found.
